metadata.title: Why an image model's burned-in captions hallucinate but an ASR endpoint's don't
metadata.topic: Subtitle accuracy; when to offload to specialized tools
metadata.purpose: The dedicated endpoint extracts the actual audio, runs ASR for word-level timing, and renders with a subtitle engine grounded in real speech. Image models can't hear audio — they guess captions; ASR + rendering sees.
metadata.clock: narration (Kokoro af_kore) — master clock; conform each beat to measured audio
metadata.exclusions: the video model's pipeline, ASR API internals, other subtitle tools
metadata.visual_object: Two stacked video frames — top: AI-hallucinated misaligned captions; bottom: real ASR captions perfectly synced.
metadata.hook: A video model can burn captions from a prompt, but they hallucinate — wrong words, wrong timing, paraphrased dialogue.

beats.narration_text: A prompt renders "beutiful product" over audio that says "beautiful product." Rerun through a dedicated subtitle endpoint and every word matches at word-level timing.
beats.scaffold_note: from Key case — cold open on the concrete instance

beats.narration_text: Prompt-burned subtitles should be the convenient path; this case shows systematic hallucination — why does delegating to a specialized tool fix it?
A video model can burn captions from a prompt, but they hallucinate — wrong words, wrong timing, paraphrased dialogue.

The dedicated endpoint extracts the actual audio, runs ASR for word-level timing, and renders with a subtitle engine grounded in real speech. Image models can't hear audio — they guess captions; ASR + rendering sees.
beats.visual: Two stacked video frames — top: AI-hallucinated misaligned captions; bottom: real ASR captions perfectly synced.
beats.scaffold_note: the resolving mechanism; orbits the visual object

beats.narration_text: A 15s reel. Prompt-burned: captions lag speech by 2 frames and skip a sentence. ASR endpoint: word-on-word, animated per word, zero hallucination.

The dedicated endpoint extracts the actual audio, runs ASR for word-level timing, and renders with a subtitle engine grounded in real speech. Image models can't hear audio — they guess captions; ASR + rendering sees.
